political.s: J.B. Hunt made employee/PAC contributions of $120,199 in 2024 and reported no direct lobbying expenses. The company does not make direct corporate political contributions but participates in trade associations including the American Trucking Association.

charity.s: J.B. Hunt donated over $6.3 million in company and employee contributions in 2021 across four pillars: crisis management, education, healthcare, and veteran support. The company supports educational initiatives including partnerships with University of Arkansas and local classroom supply donations.
charity.focus: Healthcare, veteran support, education, crisis management

environment.s: J.B. Hunt set an ambitious goal to reduce carbon emission intensity by 32% by 2034 from 2019 baseline and has achieved 16% reduction as of 2024. The company earned inclusion in the Dow Jones Best-in-Class Indices and has received EPA SmartWay Excellence Award for 10 consecutive years.

labor.s: J.B. Hunt faces mixed labor record with some settlements regarding California driver compensation issues and piece-rate pay disputes, though the company implemented corrective pay plans. The company achieved record safety performance in 2024 with declining injury rates and comprehensive safety training programs.

dei.s: J.B. Hunt created an Inclusion Office in 2021 and Inclusion Council in 2022 to lead enterprise-wide diversity and inclusion efforts. The company provides diversity funding to University of Arkansas and ranks in top 10% for diversity scores among large companies.

animals.s: Not applicable to transportation and logistics company

guns.s: Not applicable to transportation and logistics company

privacy.s: J.B. Hunt requires employees to comply with data privacy laws including HIPAA for employee records and maintains confidentiality policies. Limited public information available on comprehensive data privacy practices.

execPay.s: J.B. Hunt CEO Shelley Simpson earned $11.3 million in total compensation in 2024. The company reported a CEO-to-median-employee pay ratio of 101:1 in 2023, indicating significant disparity between executive and worker compensation.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
